channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Iran says it will ‘not hesitate’ to defend itself after latest clashes
item.description: Iran’s Foreign Ministry said overnight clashes in the Gulf between Tehran and Washington showed how Iran will respond.

item.title: After a Democrat standoff, Trump signs $70bn immigration enforcement bill
item.description: Immigrant advocates warn the funding bill offers few guardrails for ICE and CBP amid Trump's mass deportation campaign.

item.title: Trump warns of more attacks on Iran after US Apache helicopter downing
item.description: Trump warns of more attacks on Iran after US Apache helicopter downing

item.title: US military chief Hegseth warns Cuba against acquiring military arms
item.description: Hegseth's visit to Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, comes as the Trump administration increases pressure against Cuba's government.

item.title: UK PM Starmer slams ‘unjustified’ anti-immigration violence in Belfast
item.description: Anti-immigrant violence swept through Belfast, with homes and vehicles torched following a knife attack.

item.title: Is Fortress Europe becoming more strict?
item.description: The new EU's Pact on Migration and Asylum is the most sweeping overhaul of European asylum law in decades.

item.title: US inflation hits new three-year high amid energy price surge
item.description: US markets stumbled as inflation data sparked fears of interest rate rises in the coming months.

item.title: LIVE: Portugal vs Nigeria – World Cup 2026 international friendly
item.description: Follow our live build-up, with full team news coverage, ahead of our text commentary stream of the World Cup warm-up.

item.title: Mass shooting with at least 10 attackers in Johannesburg
item.description: A manhunt is underway for at least 10 suspects in a mass shooting that left 12 people dead near Johannesburg.

item.title: The Light Within | Ep 5 – El Salvador
item.description: In a Salvadoran prison, young women from rival gangs learn to co-exist and prepare for life beyond penitentiary walls.

item.title: Trump hardens tone against Iran, says ‘may keep going’ with strikes
item.description: US president claims Iran is 'completely defeated' and renews threats to bomb the country's civilian infrastructure.

item.title: US diplomat found dead in Myanmar, Thai woman in custody
item.description: US State Department confirms the fatality, but refuses to provide more information about the person's death in Yangon.

item.title: Bill Gates appears before Congress to testify over Epstein files
item.description: Microsoft founder Bill Gates appeared before Congress to testify over Epstein files

item.title: Sanctions on settlers not enough: Target Israeli gov’t, say campaigners
item.description: Campaigners argue that sanctions reflect a need to manage public anger rather than a genuine shift in state policy.

item.title: The myth of white Argentina still shapes the nation
item.description: Milei is deepening a state tradition that has equated whiteness with civilisation since its inception.

item.title: What are Lebanon’s most important political parties?
item.description: Lebanon's many powerful parties reflect the country's social, sectarian, and political divides.

item.title: Eight red cards shown to Brazil in fiery friendly against US women
item.description: Four Brazilian players and four members of their coaching staff were sent off during a dramatic encounter in Fortaleza.

item.title: US bombs Iran’s water facilities: Why that’s so significant
item.description: The US struck several sites in Iran, saying it retaliated against the downing of a US helicopter in the Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Mexico vs South Africa: World Cup group match – teams, start and lineups
item.description: As the tournament kicks off, cohosts Mexico face South Africa in a replay of the 2010 edition's opening game.

item.title: West Bank ethnic cleansing, settler attacks Israel’s state policy: Amnesty
item.description: Israel uses war crimes to accelerate 'annexation agenda and settlement expansion' in occupied land, rights group says.

item.title: 7-month-old baby shot by Israeli soldier in Hebron
item.description: An Israeli soldier fired at a Palestinian family’s car in Hebron, fatally shooting a seven‑month‑old in the head.

item.title: Ukraine-made ‘Flamingo’ missile hits target 900 km inside Russia
item.description: Video shows a Ukrainian-made ‘FP-5 Flamingo’ cruise missile flying deep inside Russia.

item.title: Pakistan military helicopter crashes in Kashmir, all on board killed
item.description: There has been a heavy security presence in Muzaffarabad amid protests in Pakistan-administered Kashmir in recent days.

item.title: Kashmiri rights activist wins partial court victory but remains behind bars
item.description: The Delhi High Court grants bail to Kashmiri rights activist Khurram Parvez, jailed in India for nearly five years.

item.title: Hero’s welcome for Somali referee denied entry to US World Cup tournament
item.description: The first Somali picked to referee a World Cup rose from Mogadishu's pitches, only to be barred at the US border.
